title: AI Governance Requirements
description: Per-control requirements organized in three columns: legal/regulatory, technical, and governance/organizational.

legal: Establish AI governance body with defined authority (EU Art. 9, 17)
Document AI governance roles and responsibilities (ISO 5.3)
Define scope of AI activities within governance framework
technical: Implement governance tooling for AI system inventory
Maintain version-controlled governance documentation
governance: Appoint AI governance lead with board-level reporting (NGAIGE Accountability)
Conduct annual governance framework review
Establish governance committee with cross-functional representation

legal: Develop AI-specific policies compliant with applicable regulations (EU Art. 9)
Define prohibited AI practices aligned with EU AI Act Art. 5
Ensure policies address lawful basis for AI data processing
technical: Implement policy version control and distribution system
Automate policy compliance checks in AI development pipelines
Maintain policy acknowledgment tracking system
governance: Establish policy review cycle with defined frequency (NGAIGE Accountability)
Define AI risk appetite statement approved by board
Align AI policies with organizational code of ethics (ISO 5.2, A.2.2)

legal: Conduct risk assessment before AI system deployment (EU Art. 9)
Classify AI systems by risk level (EU Art. 6)
Document risk assessment results and mitigation measures
technical: Implement risk scoring methodology aligned with NIST MAP function
Automate risk monitoring dashboards
Integrate risk assessment into CI/CD pipelines
governance: Define risk appetite and tolerance levels for AI systems (NIST GOVERN-1.3)
Establish risk escalation procedures
Review risk assessments at defined intervals (ISO 6.1)

legal: Classify AI systems per EU AI Act risk tiers: prohibited, high-risk, limited, minimal (Art. 6, 7)
Document classification rationale with reference to Annex III use cases
Reassess classification when system scope or deployment context changes
technical: Implement automated classification questionnaire integrated with AI inventory
Maintain machine-readable risk tier labels in model registry
Trigger control requirements automatically based on classification tier
governance: Define organizational risk classification criteria aligned with EU AI Act and NGAIGE (Reliability)
Establish classification review board or delegated authority
Conduct periodic reclassification reviews (NIST MAP-1.5)

legal: Ensure training data complies with data protection law (EU Art. 10)
Document data provenance and lawful basis for processing
Respect IP rights in training data (EU Art. 53 for GPAI)
technical: Implement data lineage tracking and cataloging
Automate data quality checks (completeness, accuracy, representativeness)
Deploy bias detection tools on training datasets
governance: Define data governance policies for AI training data (NGAIGE Privacy)
Establish data steward roles for AI datasets
Conduct regular data quality audits (NIST MAP-2.1)

legal: Conduct DPIA before processing personal data in AI systems (EU Art. 10, GDPR Art. 35)
Implement data minimization and purpose limitation for AI training data
Ensure lawful basis for all personal data processing in AI pipelines
technical: Deploy anonymization and pseudonymization techniques for training data
Implement privacy-preserving ML techniques (federated learning, differential privacy)
Automate data retention and deletion policies for AI datasets
governance: Appoint DPO oversight for AI data processing activities (NGAIGE Privacy)
Establish consent management processes for AI training data collection
Conduct regular privacy audits of AI systems (ISO A.7.4, A.8.4)

legal: Validate AI system accuracy and robustness (EU Art. 15)
Document validation methodology and benchmark results
Re-validate after significant changes
technical: Implement holdout test sets and cross-validation
Conduct adversarial robustness testing
Benchmark against established performance metrics
governance: Define validation criteria and acceptance thresholds (ISO A.6.2.3)
Establish independent validation team
Maintain validation records for audit (NIST MEASURE-2.5)

legal: Validate AI system accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity (EU Art. 15)
Document testing methodology and results for regulatory inspection
Re-test after significant model or data changes
technical: Implement automated test suites covering accuracy, fairness, and robustness
Perform adversarial testing and red-teaming before deployment
Integrate testing gates into CI/CD pipelines with pass/fail thresholds
governance: Define test acceptance criteria proportionate to risk level (NGAIGE Reliability)
Establish independent testing team separate from development
Maintain test records for audit trail (NIST MEASURE-1.1, MEASURE-2.1)

legal: Obtain conformity assessment for high-risk AI before market placement (EU Art. 43)
Document deployment approval decision with sign-off authority
Validate production environment meets technical requirements (EU Art. 9)
technical: Implement deployment readiness checklist covering all technical prerequisites
Deploy canary or staged rollout with automated rollback capability
Validate model performance in production environment before full release
governance: Define multi-stakeholder deployment approval gate (ISO 8.1)
Require sign-off from risk, legal, and technical leads before deployment
Establish post-deployment monitoring plan as deployment prerequisite (NIST MANAGE-1.1)

legal: Disclose AI system use to affected individuals (EU Art. 50)
Provide right to explanation for automated decisions (EU Art. 85)
Label AI-generated content including deepfakes (EU Art. 50)
technical: Implement explainability methods (SHAP, LIME, attention visualization)
Build user-facing explanation interfaces
Automate AI content labeling and watermarking
governance: Define transparency policies by AI system category (NGAIGE Transparency)
Publish AI system registry or inventory
Train staff on transparency obligations (ISO A.4.3-A.4.5)

legal: Provide meaningful explanations for automated decisions affecting individuals (EU Art. 13, 86)
Ensure explanations are proportionate to risk level and comprehensible to target audience
Enable affected individuals to contest AI-driven decisions with human review
technical: Implement local explanation methods (SHAP, LIME) for individual decision explanations
Deploy global explanation methods (feature importance, partial dependence) for model-level understanding
Build user-facing explanation interfaces with plain-language summaries
governance: Define explainability requirements tiered by AI system risk level (NGAIGE Transparency)
Test explanations with representative end users for comprehensibility
Document explainability trade-offs between accuracy and interpretability (ISO A.4.5)

legal: Implement human oversight with ability to override AI decisions (EU Art. 14)
Ensure human operators can interrupt AI system operation
Document oversight procedures and escalation paths
technical: Build human-in-the-loop decision workflows
Implement kill switches and override controls
Provide real-time monitoring dashboards for operators
governance: Define human oversight requirements per risk level (NGAIGE Reliability)
Train human operators on AI system limitations
Establish escalation procedures for AI failures (NIST MANAGE-2.4)

legal: Test for bias across protected characteristics (EU Art. 10)
Document bias testing methodology and results
Implement corrective measures for identified bias
technical: Deploy fairness metrics (demographic parity, equalized odds, calibration)
Implement bias detection in model validation pipeline
Use debiasing techniques (resampling, adversarial debiasing, post-processing)
governance: Define acceptable fairness thresholds per use case (NGAIGE Fairness)
Establish diverse review panels for bias assessment
Conduct regular bias audits (NIST MEASURE-2.6, 2.11)

legal: Assess differential impact on protected groups before AI deployment (EU Art. 9, 27)
Document identified risks of discrimination or exclusion with mitigation measures
Engage affected communities in assessment process where feasible
technical: Implement quantitative fairness impact metrics across demographic groups
Deploy intersectional analysis tools to detect compounding disadvantages
Automate fairness impact scoring integrated with model validation pipeline
governance: Define fairness assessment criteria aligned with organizational values and NGAIGE (Fairness, Inclusiveness)
Establish diverse review panels including affected community representatives
Conduct fairness impact reassessments when model or deployment context changes (NIST MAP-2.3, MAP-5.1)

legal: Test AI systems for cybersecurity vulnerabilities (EU Art. 15)
Document security testing methodology and results for regulatory inspection
Assess and mitigate adversarial attack risks specific to AI systems
technical: Conduct adversarial robustness testing (evasion, poisoning, extraction attacks)
Perform prompt injection and jailbreak testing for generative AI systems
Implement model integrity verification and tamper detection
governance: Define AI security testing requirements proportionate to risk level (NGAIGE Privacy-Security)
Integrate AI security testing into existing cybersecurity program
Conduct periodic AI-specific penetration testing (NIST MANAGE-2.3, ISO A.8.2)

legal: Restrict access to AI systems and training data to authorized personnel (EU Art. 15)
Implement logging of all access to AI models, training data, and inference endpoints
Ensure access controls comply with data protection requirements
technical: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) for AI models, datasets, and APIs
Secure inference endpoints with authentication and rate limiting
Deploy model access monitoring with anomaly detection
governance: Define access control policies specific to AI assets (NGAIGE Privacy-Security, Reliability)
Conduct periodic access reviews for AI systems and data
Integrate AI access controls into enterprise IAM framework (NIST MANAGE-2.1, ISO A.8.1)

legal: Maintain quality management system for AI (EU Art. 17)
Support external audit and conformity assessment (EU Art. 43)
Retain audit logs for minimum required period
technical: Implement comprehensive logging for AI decisions (ISO A.9.4)
Automate audit trail generation
Provide audit data export capabilities
governance: Schedule regular internal AI audits (ISO 9.2)
Engage independent third-party auditors
Track audit findings and corrective actions (NGAIGE Accountability)

legal: Designate authorized representative for AI systems where required (EU Art. 16, 26)
Document accountability chains for AI system outcomes
Ensure deployers fulfill obligations distinct from providers (EU Art. 26)
technical: Implement RACI tracking in project management and AI inventory tools
Automate responsibility notifications for lifecycle events (deployment, incidents, reviews)
Maintain machine-readable responsibility metadata in model registry
governance: Define RACI matrix covering full AI system lifecycle (NGAIGE Accountability)
Assign named individuals (not just roles) as accountable owners for each AI system
Review and update responsibility assignments when organizational changes occur (NIST GOVERN-1.2, GOVERN-2.1, ISO 5.3)

legal: Report serious incidents to authorities (EU Art. 72)
Maintain incident records for audit
Notify affected individuals of AI-related incidents
technical: Implement AI-specific incident detection and alerting
Automate incident classification and triage
Maintain rollback capability for model versions
governance: Define AI incident response procedures (NIST MANAGE-4)
Conduct post-incident reviews and root cause analysis
Update risk assessments based on incident learnings (ISO A.9.3)

legal: Implement post-market monitoring system for high-risk AI (EU Art. 72)
Document monitoring metrics, thresholds, and alerting criteria
Maintain monitoring records for regulatory inspection
technical: Deploy model performance monitoring (accuracy, latency, throughput degradation)
Implement data drift and concept drift detection with automated alerts
Monitor fairness metrics in production with demographic breakdowns
governance: Define monitoring KPIs and thresholds for each AI system (NGAIGE Reliability)
Establish monitoring review cadence and escalation procedures
Integrate AI monitoring into enterprise observability framework (NIST MANAGE-3.2, MEASURE-1.3, ISO A.9.1)

legal: Conduct fundamental rights impact assessment for high-risk AI (EU Art. 27)
Assess impact on vulnerable groups and protected characteristics
Document mitigation measures for identified impacts
technical: Implement impact measurement frameworks
Deploy stakeholder feedback collection systems
Monitor societal impact metrics post-deployment
governance: Engage affected communities in impact assessment (UNESCO)
Publish impact assessment summaries
Review impacts at defined intervals (NIST MAP-5, OECD Principle 1)

legal: Assess environmental impact of AI training and operation (EU AI Act Recital 27)
Evaluate impact on employment and workforce displacement
Document societal impact assessment results and mitigations
technical: Implement carbon footprint tracking for AI model training and inference
Deploy workforce impact modeling tools
Monitor resource consumption metrics (compute, energy, water)
governance: Integrate AI societal impact into ESG reporting framework (NGAIGE Human Benefit, Inclusiveness)
Engage diverse stakeholders including labor representatives in impact assessment
Conduct periodic societal impact reviews aligned with organizational sustainability goals (NIST MAP-5.1, ISO A.3.5)
